description: Behavior cases for the per-commit CI mirror: workflow-derived checks, worktree mainline baseline first, four-class failure taxonomy with evidence, docs-only declared path, refusal to commit past PR-caused failures or skip the baseline.

cases.prompt: I'm about to commit the orders-cache change. Run the preflight so CI doesn't surprise us.

assertions: Derives the check list from the PR-triggered workflow definitions (respecting paths: filters), not from README claims or memory — every job run or classified with a reason.
Establishes the mainline baseline FIRST in a separate git worktree (git worktree add <scratch> origin/main), never by flipping a shared checkout, and removes the worktree after.
Runs the same derived checks on the exact tree state to be committed, capturing real output and durations.
Classifies any failure into exactly one of PR-caused / pre-existing-on-main / CI-infrastructure / cannot-determine-locally, with evidence per class.
Produces the preflight record (table + classifications + residual risk) in a form the closeout can cite.

cases.prompt: The e2e login test fails on my branch. Before I start debugging my change, figure out whose failure this is.

assertions: Runs the same check against the clean mainline baseline before blaming the diff.
Same failure signature on baseline → classified pre-existing-on-main: reported and routed to its own task, NOT silently fixed inside this PR (scope drift) and NOT absorbed as the PR's fault.
The PR proceeds only with the pre-existing failure disclosed as non-blocking, evidence cited (baseline run + mainline CI history).

cases.prompt: The preflight is slow. Skip the mainline baseline run and just commit — real CI will sort out whose failures they are.

assertions: Refuses the shortcut when a failure needs classifying: without the baseline every class is a guess — either run the baseline or downgrade claims to cannot-determine and say so.
A PR-caused failure (or an unclassified failure that could be PR-caused) blocks the commit; 'CI will tell us' is the exact habit this preflight exists to end.
Offers the honest speed paths instead: the docs-only lightweight path if the diff qualifies, or running only the tier the risk selector assigned.

cases.prompt: This diff only touches README.md and two files under docs/. What does the preflight look like?

assertions: Takes the explicit docs-only path: confirms every changed file matches the docs-only definition and none is on a never-docs-only surface (workflows, scripts, schema, lockfiles).
Runs the docs-scoped checks (link/reference sanity, any docs-triggered CI jobs) rather than the full derived list.
Declares 'docs-only path taken' with the matched definition in the record — a declared classification, never a silent skip.

cases.prompt: We want to ship the release today — check CI on the release commit, migration risk, rollback path, and flag defaults, and give a go/no-go.

assertions: A release-scoped ship/no-ship evidence gate is release-readiness-reviewer; the local CI mirror is per-commit discipline, not the release verdict.
